title: onConnect
text: onConnect Initializes the current widget when it is connected to a Document Object Model (DOM) element. onConnect(widgetElement, objectDefinition) Arguments widgetElement The DOM element to which the framework connected the current widget. objectDefinition Gives access to the objectDefinition of the object (field, occurrence, collection, or component) that the widget will be used for. Exceptions None Return value Array<Object> - An array of updater objects. Description When the framework connects the widget to a DOM element that is part of the DSP page, it calls onConnect() function to give the widget opportunity to initialize itself. The function argument is a reference to the DOM element that represents the widget in the DSP page. This function may return an array of updater objects of format: Explain this code { element: <DOM element>, event_name: <name of event>, handler: <optional: handler function> } Copy These updaters indicate to the framework which UI actions can cause a change of the widget's value. Each updater indicates an HTML event and a DOM element. When an event occurs on the DOM element, the framework receives the widget's value and validates it. If the updater has a handler function, the framework also executes that handler, passing the event object as its single argument. Note: The DOM event is not propagated when it causes the updater execution. With objectDefinition as the second parameter of the onConnect function of the UX widget class, you can get access to objectDefinition of the object (field, occurrence, collection, or component) that the widget is for. If the object is an occurrence or a collection, the objectDefinition parameter also gives access to the definitions of child objects, like fields or inner entities. The objectDefinition parameter typically gives access to properties that were defined for the object in the IDE's Property Inspector. The objectDefinition contains the following interfaces: getName() : Returns the fully qualified name of the object, such as MYFIELD.MYENT.MYMODEL for a field, or MYENT.MYMODEL for an entity. getShortName() : Returns the unqualified name of the object, such as MYFIELD for a field or MYENT for an entity. getType() : Returns the objectDefinition type. Note: At definition level, the concept of occurrence and collection do not exist: field for a field-level definition. entity for an entity-level definition. component for a component-level definition. getWidgetClass() : Returns the name of the field-level widget class, such as specified by the widget_class property of the physical widget definition in web.ini . This function is available only if the objectDefinition type is field. getOccurrenceWidgetClass() : Returns the name of the occurrence widget class as specified by the occurrence_widget_class property of the physical widget definition in web.ini . This function is available only if the objectDefinition type is entity. getCollectionWidgetClass() : Returns the name of the collection widget class as specified by the collection_widget_class property of the physical widget definition in web.ini . This function is available only if the objectDefinition type is entity. getComponentWidgetClass : Returns the name of the component widget class as specified by the component_widget_class property of the physical widget definition in web.ini . This function is available only if the objectDefinition type is component. getPropertyNames() : Returns an array containing the names of all define-time properties of the object. getProperty(propertyName) : Returns the value of the indicated define-time property. getChildDefinitions() : Returns an array of the objectDefinition of the children of the current objectDefinition . Note: For component-level widgets, getChildDefinitions() returns the definitions of top-level entities within the component. Note: Unlike the processLayout function, the onConnect function cannot make changes to the objectDefinition parameter, nor its child definitions. For more information on the objectDefinition interface, see ObjectDefinition Methods by Widget Type . Related Links processLayout A function that informs a widget to provide a processed initial HTML layout to be used at a run-time. Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
